headline_summary: U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran have escalated into a multi-day regional conflict with direct impacts on energy infrastructure and shipping.
Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned it will fire on any ship attempting to pass; multiple vessels have been hit.
Major producers and facilities have taken precautionary shutdowns (Qatar LNG, Saudi Ras Tanura refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi Kurdistan output), tightening supply.
Container lines (Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM, MSC) are rerouting/pausing Suez/Bab el-Mandeb transits and halting Hormuz crossings; war-risk surcharges and insurer cover cancellations add costs.
Oil, European gas and shipping rates have surged; broader global markets have moved risk-off amid inflation and growth concerns.
risk_drivers: Threatened/prospective closure of the Strait of Hormuz (critical chokepoint for oil/LNG)
Direct attacks on tankers and energy infrastructure across Gulf states
Precautionary shutdowns at major oil/gas facilities (Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli gas fields, Iraqi production)
Spillover into Lebanon/Hezbollah front raising multi-theatre escalation risk
War-risk insurance withdrawal and extreme freight-rate repricing disrupting physical markets

Energy: Higher oil risk premium; potential for further upside if Hormuz closure persists or more Gulf infrastructure is hit.
European gas prices sensitive to LNG supply outages (Qatar) and shipping constraints; volatility elevated.
Refining/product spreads can widen if regional refineries/ports face disruptions (e.g., Ras Tanura, Fujairah).
FX: Risk-off flows can pressure EM FX, especially energy importers in Asia; safe-haven demand may support USD/JPY/CHF.
Gulf currencies pegs may hold but local liquidity/credit conditions could tighten if disruption extends.
Rates: Higher energy prices raise near-term inflation risk, complicating DM central bank paths and lifting term premium.
Wider credit spreads possible on geopolitical shock + growth risk; funding costs rise for shipping/energy-linked credits.
Equities: Broad risk-off with underperformance in airlines/transport/chemicals; relative support for energy producers and some defence names.
Higher input and freight costs could hit margins across global industrial and consumer supply chains.

sources.title: US and Israel launch "pre-emptive" attack against Iran
Iran vows to attack any ship trying to pass through Strait of Hormuz
Qatar LNG, Saudi refinery, Israeli oil, gas fields down due to Mideast strikes
Shipping companies divert vessels around Cape of Good Hope after strikes on Iran
Global oil and gas shipping costs surge as Iran vows to close Strait of Hormuz
Iran war enters fourth day in 'smoke and blood' as markets slide
Oil prices keep climbing amid Iran conflict, with Brent hitting 19-month high
Exclusive: India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage, sources say
Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping, oil and gas production
Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs after Hezbollah attacks

events.title: U.S. and Israel launch 'pre-emptive' strikes on Iran
events.implication: Direct state-on-state escalation raises probability of retaliation against regional bases, infrastructure, and shipping; immediate geopolitical risk premium.

events.title: Iranian retaliation includes missile barrages at Gulf neighbours hosting U.S. bases
events.implication: Raises risk to regional energy hubs and military installations; increases chance of further disruption and risk-off market moves.

events.title: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens to fire on transiting ships
events.implication: Threatens ~20% of global oil consumption flows and significant LNG volumes; drives crude/gas volatility, freight spikes, and inflation risk.

events.title: Shipping through Hormuz grinds to near halt as ships are hit and insurers cancel war-risk cover
events.implication: Physical crude/LNG logistics disrupted; higher delivered energy costs and potential shortages for import-dependent regions.

events.title: Global oil and LNG shipping costs surge to record levels amid Hormuz disruption
events.implication: Freight and insurance repricing transmits shock to end-user energy prices and broad inflation expectations.

events.title: Maersk pauses Trans-Suez sailings and multiple lines reroute around Africa after Iran strikes escalation
events.implication: Longer transit times and higher freight costs tighten global supply chains; increases delivered commodity prices.

events.title: Qatar halts LNG production after strikes on Ras Laffan and Mesaieed industrial zone
events.implication: Removes a major swing supplier (~20% of global LNG); spikes European/Asian gas benchmarks and tightens power/industrial fuel markets.

events.title: Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura refinery suspends operations after drone strike (precautionary shutdown)
events.implication: Adds to refined product and export-terminal risk; supports higher crude/product spreads and regional supply tightness.

events.title: Most oil production in Iraqi Kurdistan halts as operators stop output (precaution)
events.implication: Reduces regional supply and adds to export uncertainty; can tighten Mediterranean crude grades and support Brent.

events.title: Major Israeli gas fields, including Leviathan, go offline; exports to Egypt throttled
events.implication: Regional gas balance worsens; increases LNG demand/price support and can affect Egypt power/industry.

events.title: Fuel tank at Oman's Duqm port hit; fire breaks out
events.implication: Highlights spillover risk to alternative logistics hubs; supports freight/insurance premiums.

events.title: Fire reported at UAE's Fujairah oil hub amid regional strikes
events.implication: Risk to a key bunkering/storage hub increases product and shipping tightness; supports oil and refined product risk premia.

events.title: Iraq's Kirkuk crude loadings at Turkey's Ceyhan port stop
events.implication: Adds another export constraint into Mediterranean markets; supports regional crude differentials.

events.title: India begins cutting gas supplies to industries in anticipation of reduced Middle East LNG (Qatar outage)
events.implication: Second-round demand destruction/rationing signals physical tightness; may transmit into industrial output and EM macro stress.

events.title: Asian importers face disrupted Middle East oil supply as vessels bottleneck and transport costs rise
events.implication: Energy-importer terms-of-trade shock can pressure current accounts, FX, and domestic inflation; raises recession tail risk.

events.title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs after Hezbollah launches missiles and drones toward Israel
events.implication: Opens/expands a Lebanon front alongside Iran conflict; increases chance of prolonged regional instability and broader asset risk-off.

links.from: U.S. and Israel launch 'pre-emptive' strikes on Iran
links.to: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens to fire on transiting ships
links.why: Reuters reports Hormuz closure threat was triggered by U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.

links.from: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens to fire on transiting ships
links.to: Shipping through Hormuz grinds to near halt as ships are hit and insurers cancel war-risk cover
links.why: Closure threat and attacks led vessels to halt/anchor and insurers to withdraw cover, disrupting flows.

links.from: Shipping through Hormuz grinds to near halt as ships are hit and insurers cancel war-risk cover
links.to: Global oil and LNG shipping costs surge to record levels amid Hormuz disruption
links.why: Reuters notes freight rates and war-risk pricing spiked as traffic halted and attacks occurred.

links.from: Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz closed and threatens to fire on transiting ships
links.why: Hormuz is a major chokepoint for oil/LNG; closure risk increases crude supply risk premium.

links.from: Qatar halts LNG production after strikes on Ras Laffan and Mesaieed industrial zone
links.why: Broader energy supply shock (gas/LNG) tends to support oil-linked risk premia and cross-commodity substitution.

links.from: Global oil and LNG shipping costs surge to record levels amid Hormuz disruption
links.why: Higher delivered energy/freight costs raise inflation risk and can widen credit spreads for transport/industrial sectors.

links.from: Asian importers face disrupted Middle East oil supply as vessels bottleneck and transport costs rise
links.why: Higher import bills and supply disruptions worsen trade balances for energy importers, pressuring EM FX.

links.from: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs after Hezbollah launches missiles and drones toward Israel
links.why: A widening multi-front conflict raises tail risk, typically pushing investors into safe assets.
